steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence (“Boxer crabs use stinging anemones to defend against attack”) within the whole passage. The preceding sentence establishes a claim: “Other animals also find tools helpful.” The key task is identifying how the underlined sentence relates to that claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It explains that humans use tools for many different tasks.’: The underlined sentence is about boxer crabs, not humans. The passage has already shifted away from humans. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It provides one example of tool use in a nonhuman animal.’: Boxer crabs are a nonhuman animal, and using anemones for defense is precisely one instance of tool use. This matches the sentence’s role as concrete support for the preceding claim. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘It argues that boxer crabs use tools more often than other animals do.’: The sentence makes no comparison of frequency between species. No such argument appears in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It emphasizes that boxer crabs are smarter than birds.’: The passage never compares the intelligence of boxer crabs and birds; birds are introduced separately as a second example later. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
